data.title: Pacira Faces Board Fight As Activist Investor Criticizes CEO Pay, Missed Earnings
data.description:
DOMA Perpetual Capital Management on Wednesday nominated three candidates to Pacira BioSciences Inc.s (NASDAQ:PCRX) board, citing concerns over the company’s performance and management oversight, adding pressure as broader markets edged lower.

Inadequate Financial Controls at Pacira BioSciences

DOMA Perpetual, which holds approximately 7.1% of Pacira’s outstanding shares, has expressed that the current board has failed to provide adequate financial controls and oversight, contributing to a significant decline in the stock price over the years.

The firm highlighted that under CEO Frank Lee, the stock has fallen 30%, and it has consistently missed earnings expectations while management compensation has soared.

Need To Change Leadership

In its statement, DOMA emphasized the need for a change in leadership to restore shareholder confidence and maximize returns.

“Management executive compensation is unsustainable, currently approaching 7% of the Firm’s entire market capitalization,” DOMA said in a press release on Wednesday.

The firm believes that the board should engage bankers to explore a sale of the company and discontinue future acquisitions, arguing that the current management has proven incapable of meeting performance-based goals.

What Pacira Has To Say

Pacira BioSciences Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 1.59% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 2.63% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term strength.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 3.58% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 53.42, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at 0.1205, above its signal line at 0.1046, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $23.00
  • Key Support: $22.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $34.75. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $38.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $30.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Barclays: Initiated with Equal-Weight (Target $27.00) (Dec. 9, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Pacira BioSciences, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 59.78 — The stock is trading at a moderate valuation compared to peers.
  • Quality: 36.88 — Indicates some concerns regarding the company’s operational effectiveness.
  • Momentum: 22.85 — Suggests the stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Pacira BioSciences’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Value score indicates some potential for recovery, the low Momentum score suggests that the stock is currently struggling to gain traction in the market.

PCRX Stock Price Activity: Pacira BioSciences shares were up 0.18% at $22.87 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Top 2 Consumer Stocks That May Keep You Up At Night This Quarter
data.description:
As of March 12, 2026, two stocks in the consumer discretionary sector could be flashing a real warning to investors who value momentum as a key criteria in their trading decisions.

The RSI is a momentum indicator, which compares a stock’s strength on days when prices go up to its strength on days when prices go down. When compared to a stock’s price action, it can give traders a better sense of how a stock may perform in the short term. An asset is typically considered overbought when the RSI is above 70, according to Benzinga Pro.

Here's the latest list of major overbought players in this sector.

data.title: Why Is Firefly Aerospace Stock Soaring Thursday?
data.description:
Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ:FLY) shares moved higher in premarket trading on Thursday after the company reported a successful launch of its Alpha Flight 7 mission.

Alpha Flight 7 Mission Successfully Reaches Orbit

The Alpha Flight 7 mission validated several upgrades to the Alpha rocket, including a new avionics suite and an enhanced thermal protection system. These improvements are part of Firefly's preparations for its upcoming Block II configuration.

The launch also highlighted Firefly's ongoing focus on improving reliability and manufacturability. The company is now working to complete the final milestones for Alpha Flight 8, which will incorporate the latest upgrades and launch in the near future.

Technical Analysis

Firefly Aerospace stock trades above its 20-day SMA but below its 50-day and 100-day SMAs. This signals a mixed short-term trend. Over the past year, the stock has fallen 65.87%, showing significant volatility.

The RSI stands at 46.91, indicating neutral momentum and showing the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, the MACD is at -1.0007, with its signal line at -1.1558, indicating a bullish signal as the MACD is above the signal line.

The combination of a neutral RSI and a bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while there are some positive signals, the stock is still struggling to gain sustained upward momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $24.50
  • Key Support: $21.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $29.20. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Raises Target to $32.00) (Jan. 20)
  • Keybanc: Initiated with Sector Weight (Dec. 19, 2025)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Lowers Target to $28.00) (Nov. 25, 2025)

Top ETF Exposure

  • Truth Social American Next Frontiers ETF (NYSE:TSNF): 1.03% Weight

Significance: Because FLY carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

FLY Stock Price Activity: Firefly Aerospace shares were up 14.32% at $23.55 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Why Trump Wants Greenland So Badly—What's Under The Ice Could Explain It
data.description:
President Donald Trump‘s desire to buy Greenland sounded like a geopolitical punchline, at first. After all, Denmark still oversees the island’s foreign affairs and defense. Then Trump’s comments became a hard-line policy, one that makes Greenlanders uneasy.

Greenland Oil And Global Energy Security

Robert Price, CEO of March GL and incoming chief of Greenland Energy Company, argues that such chokepoints expose a deeper structural problem.

"Markets react to headlines, but they often underestimate how fragile global energy flows really are," Price said. Western economies remain deeply dependent on politically volatile regions for oil supply, he added—making new reserves in stable jurisdictions increasingly valuable.

That's where Greenland, perhaps to its dismay, enters the conversation.

Beneath Greenland Ice: A 13 Billion Barrel Oil Basin

According to Price, the Jameson Land Basin in eastern Greenland could hold roughly 13 billion barrels of oil resource potential, based on independent geological evaluations. If even a fraction of that estimate proves recoverable, it would rank among the largest undeveloped onshore petroleum systems in the Arctic.

Price says the basin stands out not just for its size, but for how much groundwork has already been done.

More than $275 million in historical exploration and seismic studies have mapped the region, identifying over 50 potential drilling targets. The basin also shows natural oil and gas seeps with biomarker signatures similar to those found in prolific fields in the Norwegian sector of the North Sea.

In scale, Price says the basin could resemble Alaska's legendary Prudhoe Bay Oil Field, one of North America's largest discoveries.

That geological promise is why Pelican Acquisition Corporation is planning a merger that would take the project public as Greenland Energy Company, giving investors exposure to what Price calls "one of the Arctic's largest untapped onshore oil provinces."

Why Greenland Suddenly Matters

Arctic oil exploration remains limited by high costs and low crude prices. But the energy landscape has shifted.

Short-cycle U.S. shale has acted as a supply buffer for years, yet operators now face rising costs and shrinking prime drilling inventory. At the same time, geopolitical shocks—from U.S. military intervention in the Middle East to shipping disruptions—continue to remind markets how vulnerable global oil flows remain.

That dynamic is driving renewed interest in long-cycle conventional resources that could supply energy for decades.

Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen, in January, said: "We are now facing a geopolitical crisis, and if we have to choose between the United States and Denmark here and now, we choose Denmark."

Image created using artificial intelligence via ChatGPT.

data.title: GE Aerospace, Palantir Double Down On AI To Keep US Warplanes Mission-Ready
data.description:
The companies are deploying agentic artificial intelligence tools to predict equipment failures, streamline supply chains, and support maintenance, repair, and overhaul operations.

Program Builds on T-38 Engine Pilot

The collaboration began with a program focused on improving readiness for the Air Force’s T-38 trainer jets powered by the J85 engine. A 2024 pilot project provided better visibility into parts demand and shortages.

GE Aerospace now uses Palantir’s Artificial Intelligence Platform across select supply chain functions, including sourcing, fulfillment and maintenance operations, to automate repetitive tasks and improve decision-making.

“Meeting today’s readiness demands requires both proven propulsion and smarter use of data,” said Amy Gowder, president and CEO of Defense and Systems for GE Aerospace.

Palantir’s Mike Gallagher said the partnership helps “keep more aircraft available and more airmen trained.”

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 2.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 4% above its 100-day SMA, indicating a mixed short-term trend.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 66.74%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 48.65, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD shows a value of 2.7428, with a signal line at 5.2225, indicating bearish pressure as the MACD is below the signal line.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum for the stock.

  • Key Resistance: $348.50
  • Key Support: $290.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

GE Aerospace is slated to provide its next financial update on April 21, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: $1.60 (Up from $1.49)
  • Revenue Estimate: $10.63 Billion (Up from $9.94 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 40.4x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $303.79. Recent analyst moves include:

  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Raises Target to $335.00) (Jan. 26)
  • UBS: Buy (Raises Target to $374.00) (Jan. 23)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for GE Aerospace, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value Rank: 4.39 — Stock is trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality Rank: 88.86 — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum Rank: 86.25 — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: GE Aerospace’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum (86.25) confirms the strong trend, the low Value (4.39) score warns that the stock is priced for perfection—investors should ride the trend but use tight stop-losses.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because GE carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.
